perturbation.original_text: The amount payable pursuant to either of the prior two sentences (sometimes referred to as the "Section 2(a) Amount") shall be reduced by the sum of (A) 100% of any costs incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is owned by WYZZ or which is used solely in connection with the operation of WYZZ-TV, which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF, plus (B) 50% of the cost incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is not owned by WYZZ and which is used in connection with the combined operation of both Stations ("Combined Capital Equipment)", which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF.
perturbation.changed_text: The amount payable pursuant to either of the prior two sentences (sometimes referred to as the "Section 2(a) Amount") shall be reduced by the sum of (A) 50% of any costs incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is owned by WYZZ or which is used solely in connection with the operation of WYZZ-TV, which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF, plus (B) 50% of the cost incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is not owned by WYZZ and which is used in connection with the combined operation of both Stations ("Combined Capital Equipment)", which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 2(a) modifies the percentage of capital equipment costs that can be deducted from the Section 2(a) Amount. Originally, 100% of WYZZ-owned equipment costs could be deducted, now it is changed to 50%. This creates an in-text contradiction because later exhibits and sections may rely on the original 100% deduction, creating disputes over financial responsibilities and calculations of the BCF (Broadcast Cash Flow), which is defined based on these expenses. This inconsistency can lead to uncertainty in enforcement of financial obligations under the agreement. The term 'capital equipment' is being conflicted in its applicability due to the cost reduction amount that can be applied, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within seventy-five (75) days following the end of each month of a calendar year during the Term that "BCF" (as defined below) for such month, when combined with BCF for all prior months during such calendar year (other than any month which is outside the Term) is less than the Minimum BCF, Nexstar shall pay a fee to WYZZ in an amount equal to thirty-five percent (35%) of BCF for such month. Within seventy-five (75) days following the end of each month of a calendar year during the Term that BCF for such month, when combined with BCF for all prior months of such calendar year (other than any month which is outside the Term) is greater than the Minimum BCF, Nexstar shall pay a fee to WYZZ in an amount equal to 50% of BCF for such month; provided, in the first month of each calendar year in which this sentence applies, the fee with respect to the portion of BCF for such month which, when combined with BCF for all prior months during such year (other than any month which is outside the Term) is exactly equal to the Minimum BCF, shall equal thirty-five percent (35%) of such portion rather than fifty percent (50%). The amount payable pursuant to either of the prior two sentences (sometimes referred to as the "Section 2(a) Amount") shall be reduced by the sum of (A) 50% of any costs incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is owned by WYZZ or which is used solely in connection with the operation of WYZZ-TV, which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF, plus (B) 50% of the cost incurred by Nexstar during the applicable month in maintaining, replacing or purchasing capital equipment which is not owned by WYZZ and which is used in connection with the combined operation of both Stations ("Combined Capital Equipment)", which costs have not otherwise been deducted in calculating BCF.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the percentage of deductible costs for WYZZ-owned capital equipment from 100% to 50%. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which also defines the 'Section 2(a) Amount' and relies on the deduction of costs. The change creates ambiguity and inconsistency as the BCF calculation, defined based on deductible expenses, is now subject to different interpretations, affecting the financial obligations and payments between Nexstar and WYZZ. Since the contradicted text is also the changed text, the discrepancy is even more prominent and introduces uncertainty regarding the financial responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "BCF" is defined as Net Income of the Stations, determined on a basis in accordance with GAAP, plus the sum of (X) (in each case to the extent deducted in calculating Net Income) (i) depreciation expense (ii) amortization expense (including amortization of program assets and amortization of deferred and stock based compensation) (iii) interest expense (iv) corporate overhead or management fees (v) income tax expense (vi) non-cash losses or expenses (including losses on disposals of assets and trade/barter expenses) (vii) Section 2(a) Amount expenses pursuant to Section 2(a) hereof (viii) any expenses (other than 4 electric costs relating to the digital transmission of either Station's primary commercial broadcast signal) related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" (ix) costs or expenses arising from any claims relating to the period prior to the Effective Date, and (x) any expenses or costs identified on Exhibit D hereto less the sum of (Y) (i) cash payments for program contract rights relating to the Stations (ii) payments made by Nexstar to WYZZ pursuant to clause (b) of this Section 2, to the extent not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Income, (iii) the aggregate fair market value rent (as set forth on Exhibit C hereto) of any real property owned by either Nexstar or WYZZ (or any affiliate of either) and used by the Stations to the extent the Stations are not using corresponding real property owned by each of the parties hereto (or any affiliate thereof) and which is not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Income, (iv) any other rental income earned by either WYZZ or Nexstar from real property owned or leased thereby to the extent included in calculating Net Income (v) interest income to the extent included in calculating Net Income (vi) any rent paid with respect to any capital leases of the Stations (vii ) any revenues related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" and (viii) any non-cash gains or revenues (including gains on disposals of assets and trade/barter revenue).
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "BCF" is defined as Net Profit of the Stations, determined on a basis in accordance with GAAP, plus the sum of (X) (in each case to the extent deducted in calculating Net Profit) (i) depreciation expense (ii) amortization expense (including amortization of program assets and amortization of deferred and stock based compensation) (iii) interest expense (iv) corporate overhead or management fees (v) income tax expense (vi) non-cash losses or expenses (including losses on disposals of assets and trade/barter expenses) (vii) Section 2(a) Amount expenses pursuant to Section 2(a) hereof (viii) any expenses (other than 4 electric costs relating to the digital transmission of either Station's primary commercial broadcast signal) related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" (ix) costs or expenses arising from any claims relating to the period prior to the Effective Date, and (x) any expenses or costs identified on Exhibit D hereto less the sum of (Y) (i) cash payments for program contract rights relating to the Stations (ii) payments made by Nexstar to WYZZ pursuant to clause (b) of this Section 2, to the extent not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Profit, (iii) the aggregate fair market value rent (as set forth on Exhibit C hereto) of any real property owned by either Nexstar or WYZZ (or any affiliate of either) and used by the Stations to the extent the Stations are not using corresponding real property owned by each of the parties hereto (or any affiliate thereof) and which is not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Profit, (iv) any other rental income earned by either WYZZ or Nexstar from real property owned or leased thereby to the extent included in calculating Net Profit (v) interest income to the extent included in calculating Net Profit (vi) any rent paid with respect to any capital leases of the Stations (vii ) any revenues related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" and (viii) any non-cash gains or revenues (including gains on disposals of assets and trade/barter revenue).
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Net Income" to "Net Profit" within the definition of BCF in Section 2(d), and maintaining the rest of the agreement with Net Income, it causes an in-text contradiction due to financial jargons differing from net profit, leading to different interpretations of the BCF calculation. This is because subsequent mentions of the BCF Report and related financial obligations may be interpreted using either definition. While both refer to a profit for the company, depending on where GAAP rules are applied there could be a discrepancy, and the failure to define each term can lead to multiple interpretations, and therefore uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "BCF" is defined as Net Profit of the Stations, determined on a basis in accordance with GAAP, plus the sum of (X) (in each case to the extent deducted in calculating Net Profit) (i) depreciation expense (ii) amortization expense (including amortization of program assets and amortization of deferred and stock based compensation) (iii) interest expense (iv) corporate overhead or management fees (v) income tax expense (vi) non-cash losses or expenses (including losses on disposals of assets and trade/barter expenses) (vii) Section 2(a) Amount expenses pursuant to Section 2(a) hereof (viii) any expenses (other than 4 electric costs relating to the digital transmission of either Station's primary commercial broadcast signal) related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" (ix) costs or expenses arising from any claims relating to the period prior to the Effective Date, and (x) any expenses or costs identified on Exhibit D hereto less the sum of (Y) (i) cash payments for program contract rights relating to the Stations (ii) payments made by Nexstar to WYZZ pursuant to clause (b) of this Section 2, to the extent not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Profit, (iii) the aggregate fair market value rent (as set forth on Exhibit C hereto) of any real property owned by either Nexstar or WYZZ (or any affiliate of either) and used by the Stations to the extent the Stations are not using corresponding real property owned by each of the parties hereto (or any affiliate thereof) and which is not otherwise taken into account in calculating Net Profit, (iv) any other rental income earned by either WYZZ or Nexstar from real property owned or leased thereby to the extent included in calculating Net Profit (v) interest income to the extent included in calculating Net Profit (vi) any rent paid with respect to any capital leases of the Stations (vii ) any revenues related to either Station's "DTV Spectrum" and (viii) any non-cash gains or revenues (including gains on disposals of assets and trade/barter revenue).
perturbation.justification: The change from "Net Income" to "Net Profit" in the definition of BCF, while maintaining references to "Net Income" in subsequent calculations and reporting obligations, introduces a contradiction. While both terms refer to a company's profitability, GAAP application may lead to differing calculations. This misalignment creates ambiguity and potential disputes over the BCF amount, thus a contradiction exists. The contradicted text defines BCF using "Net Profit" however this conflicts with other locations using "Net Income."

perturbation.original_text: Exhibit I contains a preliminary list of all employees, by position held by each, who are to be terminated from each of the Stations on such date as is mutually agreed by the parties on or after the Effective Time, but not later than March 1, 2002 (the "Terminated Employees"). All severance liabilities and all COBRA liabilities for any Terminated Employee terminated on or after the Effective Date shall be prorated between the Parties, in accordance with Section 4 hereof and Exhibit D hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Exhibit I contains a preliminary list of all personnel, by position held by each, who are to be terminated from each of the Stations on such date as is mutually agreed by the parties on or after the Effective Time, but not later than March 1, 2002 (the "Terminated Employees"). All severance liabilities and all COBRA liabilities for any Terminated Employee terminated on or after the Effective Date shall be prorated between the Parties, in accordance with Section 4 hereof and Exhibit D hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'employees' to 'personnel' in the first sentence of Section 11(b) introduces uncertainty. While Exhibit I refers to Terminated Employees, which strongly indicates individuals formally employed, using 'personnel' broadens the category to potentially include contractors or other non-employee workers. This creates an in-text contradiction when determining severance and COBRA liabilities, as these are typically only applicable to employees, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit I contains a preliminary list of all personnel, by position held by each, who are to be terminated from each of the Stations on such date as is mutually agreed by the parties on or after the Effective Time, but not later than March 1, 2002 (the "Terminated Employees"). All severance liabilities and all COBRA liabilities for any Terminated Employee terminated on or after the Effective Date shall be prorated between the Parties, in accordance with Section 4 hereof and Exhibit D hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical, because the change occurred on the contradicted text. The perturbation explanation suggests that changing 'employees' to 'personnel' introduces ambiguity regarding severance and COBRA liabilities. However, the contradicted text also contains 'personnel', which was a result of the change. Therefore, it's not clear that a strong contradiction has been created. The use of 'Terminated Employees' (a defined term) later in the same sentence provides context and mitigates the ambiguity.
